body:
Title: CRA 2025 T1 return aggregation lines.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package/5000-r.html
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-r/5000-r-25e.txt

CRA Form 5000-R Income Tax and Benefit Return for 2025 includes common federal return aggregation lines. Step 5 Part C line 142, line 42000, adds line 139, advanced Canada workers benefit line 41500, and special taxes line 41800 to calculate net federal tax.

Step 6 line 148, line 43500, adds amount from line 42000, CPP contributions payable on self-employment income and other earnings line 42100, employment insurance premiums payable on self-employment and other eligible earnings line 42120, social benefits repayment line 42200, and provincial or territorial tax line 42800 to calculate total payable.

Step 6 line 166, line 48200, adds total income tax deducted line 43700, refundable Quebec abatement line 44000, CPP or QPP overpayment line 44800, employment insurance overpayment line 45000, refundable medical expense supplement line 45200, Canada workers benefit line 45300, Canada training credit line 45350, multigenerational home renovation tax credit line 45355, refund of investment tax credit line 45400, Part XII.2 tax credit line 45600, employee and partner GST/HST rebate line 45700, eligible educator school supply tax credit line 46900, Canadian journalism labour tax credit line 47555, return of fuel charge proceeds to farmers tax credit line 47556, tax paid by instalments line 47600, and provincial or territorial credits line 47900 to calculate total credits.

Step 6 line 167 subtracts total credits line 48200 from total payable line 43500. If the amount is negative, it is entered on refund line 48400. If the amount is positive, it is entered on balance owing line 48500.
heading: CRA 2025 T1 Return Aggregation
